The greatest gangster moment in UFC history might belong to Jorge Masvidal — the infamous “three-piece and a soda” on Leon Edwards. But Arman Tsarukyan might be making a run at that title. After the Real American Freestyle brawl with Giorgio Petrosyan, Arman went on Ariel Helwani’s show to essentially forgive Giorgio — while maintaining that Giorgio started everything. Here’s the problem: I was there. Front row. Nobody closer. And what I saw was Arman on top after the match throwing a closed fist and a knee that ignited the riot. Now he’s reframing the narrative — calmly, charmingly, convincingly — the same way Masvidal once did. And somehow, it works. Arman is everywhere right now: grappling matches, media appearances, wrestling exhibitions, title discussions. He’ll fight anyone under any rules. That’s real. That’s endearing. But when it comes to what actually sparked the chaos… are we rewriting history? Or did I see it wrong? Watch the breakdown and decide. #ArmanTsarukyan #JorgeMasvidal #UFC #MMA #RealAmericanFreestyle 0:00 The Greatest Gangster Ever 0:23 “Three Piece and a Soda” 1:50 Arman Goes on Ariel 2:38 The Charm Factor 3:37 I Was There 4:07 What I Saw 4:48 Masvidal Comparison 5:15 Arman’s Media Run 6:01 Grappling Match Incoming 6:33 Ilia vs Gaethje Reality 7:14 My Favorite Part Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: 'Fighters are AVOIDING ME!' | Michael 'Venom' Page (UFC London Exclusive Interview)
articles.description: Michael ‘Venom’ Page joins the show ahead of his return at UFC London, where he faces Sam Patterson in front of a home crowd at the O2 Arena. MVP talks about gaining weight while filming Roadhouse 2 with Jake Gyllenhaal, revealing he walked around at nearly 240lbs, before locking in for fight camp. He also explains the frustration of trying to get bigger ranked fights in the UFC, claiming it’s been “very difficult” to find opponents, and why he even offered to fight in two weight divisions to stay active. Plus, MVP discusses fighting a training partner, his mindset going into the bout, and what a big win in London could mean for his welterweight title ambitions. FULL EPISODES @bispingpodcast Timestamps: 00:00: Weighing in at 240lbs during camp and filming Roadhouse 2 02:00 Moving back to welterweight after gaining weight 04:46 Fighting at UFC London vs Sam Patterson | What's the mindset? 06:40 History sparring Sam Petterson before his UFC fights 11:13 Difficulty getting fights at UFC middleweight and welterweight 14:30 Fighting at UFC London for for the first time / thoughts on the card 🔒 Become a Member for special Perks, Exclusive Content and ultra-exclusive Live Chats: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCDrG2_1TcVkXKXXsD6Kjwig/join #bisping #ufc #mma #mixedmartialarts #michaelbisping #ufcnews #mmanews #ufclondon #michaelvenompage

articles.title: 200 People & Total Chaos #ufc
articles.description: I’ve never seen anything like this in wrestling. Front row seat. Nearly 200 people involved. Total chaos. Then Ariel Helwani plays the clip and captions it: “Do a rematch.” And honestly… he might have nailed it. Because what we saw wasn’t just a scuffle. It was two high-level competitors who clearly have unfinished business. Emotions boiled over. The moment spiraled. And now the question isn’t what happened. It’s what do you do next? You bring them back. You calm them down. You restate the rules. You remind them what was agreed to. And you do it again. Not for chaos. Not for revenge. For clarity. If there’s real tension, settle it the right way — inside the ruleset. Wrestling has structure. It has boundaries. It has discipline. When that breaks down, the only way to fix it is to restore order and let skill decide. A rematch doesn’t reward bad behavior. It gives both men a clean slate. Because if there’s doubt about who won the moment — or how it ended — the only way to erase it is to compete again under control. Sometimes the solution isn’t punishment. It’s resolution. Subscribe for real fight breakdowns, wrestling culture analysis, and no-fluff MMA commentary. #Wrestling #MMA #ArielHelwani #FightBreakdown #Rematch #CombatSports

articles.title: Did Arman Cross the Line? #ufc
articles.description: It turns out Arman Tsarukyan is an excellent wrestler. High-level. Relentless. Technical. And it turns out Giorgi Khaibulaev is tough as an old leather boot. Durable. Competitive. The real deal. Here’s where things get interesting. Giorgi’s down. Arman’s on top. Something is said. Something happens. Arman reacts instantly. In wrestling, your hands matter. You can post. You can push. You can collar tie. You can slap with an open hand. But it must be open. The second the fist closes — it’s no longer wrestling. It’s a punch. And that’s the line. Open hand? Legal control. Closed fist? Now you’re in striking territory. In MMA, that line blurs. In pure wrestling culture, it doesn’t. So what was it? A reaction? A shove? Or a moment where emotion crossed into combat? This is the difference between grappling discipline and fight instinct. Elite competitors live on that edge — especially when tempers flare. The real question isn’t whether Arman can wrestle. We know he can. It’s whether, in that split second, the mindset shifted from control… to retaliation. That’s what makes this moment controversial. Subscribe for real fight breakdowns, technical analysis, and the stories behind MMA’s biggest moments. #ArmanTsarukyan #Wrestling #MMA #FightBreakdown #MMANews #Grappling #UFC

articles.title: There Is No ‘Big Fight’ for Islam Makhachev
articles.description: Ali Abdelaziz says there are “lots of big fights” for Islam Makhachev. No, there aren’t. And that’s not an insult — it’s a compliment. When you’re the pound-for-pound number one fighter in the world, like Islam or Jon Jones, the list of credible mega-fights shrinks fast. If it’s not Kamaru Usman, and it’s not Ian Garry, and it’s not Conor McGregor, who exactly is this massive fight supposed to be? Ilia Topuria? That only works if divisions shift — and that’s unlikely. You have to be careful as a champion and as a promotion when you promise “huge news.” Contenders can overpromise because any step up feels big. Champions don’t get that luxury. Islam is already the main event. Islam is already the belt. Islam is already the draw. Sometimes you don’t have a blockbuster — you just have the next fight. And that’s okay. But if you’re going to promise something massive, it better deliver. Let’s break down who’s actually left — and why the list might already be empty. #IslamMakhachev #UFC #MMA #KamaruUsman #IanGarry #IliaTopuria 0:00 “There Are Lots of Big Fights” 0:47 Jon Jones Comparison 1:48 Champions Can’t Overpromise 2:21 If It’s Not Usman or Garry… 3:27 You’re Out of Bullets 4:11 The White House Card Comparison 4:51 It’s Not Conor 5:24 Ilia Teasing “Big News” 6:00 The Secret Announcement Problem 7:08 You Are the Big News Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

Terms: sportsbook.draftkings.com/promos. Ends 2/15/26 at 11:59 PM ET. Sponsored by DK. There’s the Arman Tsarukyan vs. Giorgio Petrosyan brawl you saw… and then there’s the story you didn’t see. Before the chaos at Real American Freestyle, there was a silent standoff inside a single hotel workout room — one room, two opponents, and nobody willing to blink. Arman Tsarukyan walks in one door, Giorgio walks in the other, and suddenly it’s animal kingdom politics at its finest. Then the defending Olympic champion from Bulgaria steps into the center of the room and sends a message without saying a word. Fast forward to after the match and all hell breaks loose — nearly 200 people involved — and somehow my 10-year-old son decides he’s jumping the rail to break it up because Arman shook his hand earlier and Giorgio shared an elevator with him. This isn’t just about a scuffle; it’s about pride, territory, respect, and what competition really does to people — including kids watching from the front row. articles.title: Islam's Next Opponent? Think Bigger!
articles.description: Ian Garry just called out Islam Makhachev and implied the champion needed “more time.” That’s not a small accusation — and it demanded a response. Islam’s manager Ali Abdelaziz fired back, saying big talks are happening but Garry isn’t involved… and that it’s one of the biggest fights the UFC can make. That narrows the field. If we’re being honest, there’s only one mega-fight for Islam from a pure interest standpoint: Ilia Topuria. Pound-for-pound king versus undefeated wrecking machine. That’s the fight that sells arenas. But if it’s not Ilia — and Garry’s already ruled out — then who? Kamaru Usman says it’s not him. Which leaves a dangerous possibility: Arman Tsarukyan. The one contender who can legitimately make you question the ending. And you can’t have a “big fight” unless fans doubt the result. So what is Ali hinting at? Islam vs Ilia? Islam vs Arman? Or something nobody sees coming? Watch the breakdown and decide. #UFC #IslamMakhachev #IliaTopuria #IanGarry #ArmanTsarukyan #MMABad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

Token and Bonus Bets are single-use and non-withdrawable. Bet must settle by and Token expires 2/22/26. Bonus Bets expire in 7 days (168 hours). Stake removed from payout. I had a front-row seat to the Arman Tsarukyan vs. Giorgio Petrosyan match at Real American Freestyle — and what started as a wrestling bout turned into something I have never seen in my life. It began with open-hand slaps, which in wrestling can fall into a strange gray area depending on contact and referee discretion, and quickly escalated into cautions, points, and rising tension. The referee managed it perfectly in my opinion, keeping both athletes in the match despite the intensity. But when time expired and the competition technically ended, the line between sport and emotion disappeared. A push turned into a closed fist, and suddenly this wasn’t wrestling anymore. Cornermen stormed the mat. Security rushed in. Leaders from the promotion tried to intervene. Nearly 200 people were involved. Injuries happened. It was chaos. And now the question becomes — do you bury this moment and move on, or do you bring them back for a rematch and let the sport settle it the right way? Watch the full breakdown and decide for yourself. #ArmanTsarukyan #RealAmericanFreestyle #Wrestling #MMA #FightNews 0:00 I Had a Front Row Seat 0:29 The First Slap 1:09 Referee Steps In 2:37 Points Without Takedowns 3:51 Match Ends… But It’s Not Over 4:30 The Closed Fist 5:40 It Turns Into a Melee 6:58 Injuries and Fallout 7:46 Ariel Suggests a Rematch Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Stop Pushing Arman Aside #ufc
articles.description: Did Michael Bisping just suggest the BMF winner could leapfrog the division? Now we’re hearing talk of Max Holloway vs. Charles Oliveira — and the winner potentially jumping straight into a lightweight title shot. Okay. But how long are we going to push Arman Tsarukyan to the side? Arman has done everything asked of him. He’s beaten elite competition. He’s stayed active. He’s taken risks. And somehow, every time a title opportunity opens, something else slides in front of him. Superfights. BMF hype. Short-notice narratives. We’re told Islam Makhachev is coming back soon. Justin Gaethje would fight tonight if you called him. Fine. But merit has to matter at some point. It doesn’t even have to be the undisputed belt. If there are two title-level opportunities floating around — undisputed or interim — why isn’t Arman locked into one of them? At lightweight, timing is everything. And every delay costs a contender momentum, leverage, and prime years. This isn’t about disrespecting legends or superfights. It’s about competitive order. If rankings mean anything, if eliminators mean anything, if earning your shot means anything — Arman Tsarukyan shouldn’t still be waiting. The question isn’t whether he’s ready. The question is: how long do we keep pretending he isn’t next? Subscribe for real UFC lightweight breakdowns, matchmaking analysis, and fight business truth. #ArmanTsarukyan #UFC #Lightweight #MaxHolloway #CharlesOliveira #IslamMakhachev #JustinGaethje #MMANews

articles.title: Why Isn’t Strickland vs Chimaev Booked? #ufc
articles.description: Is Khamzat Chimaev a “closet champion”? Here’s what that really means. A closet champion isn’t someone holding the belt. It’s someone who holds the division. The fights don’t move unless they move. The title picture doesn’t clarify until they’re ready. Chimaev used to be one of the most transparent fighters in MMA. During his life-threatening COVID battle, he kept fans updated. He let people in. He built trust. Now? Silence. Meanwhile: Sean Strickland beats Nassourdine Imavov. Strickland beats Anthony Hernandez. Momentum says Strickland is the guy. So why isn’t Strickland vs. Chimaev confirmed? Because divisions don’t move on merit alone. They move on timing, health, leverage, and business. If Chimaev isn’t ready, the UFC waits. If the belt is tied up, contenders stall. If negotiations aren’t done, nothing gets announced. That’s power. Chimaev isn’t the official champion. But when an entire weight class feels paused until you speak — that’s championship gravity. The real question isn’t whether he’s hiding. It’s whether the UFC sees him as the biggest fight available right now. Until contracts are signed, this division is in limbo. And that tells you everything. Subscribe for real UFC matchmaking breakdowns, fight business analysis, and no-fluff MMA talk. #KhamzatChimaev #SeanStrickland #UFC #Middleweight #MMANews #FightBreakdown #MMAAnalysis

articles.title: Daniel Cormier REACTS to Alex Pereira VACATING light heavyweight title, Jiri vs. Ulberg fight
articles.description: Daniel Cormier IS BACK after Dana White made some MAJOR ANNOUNCEMENT Friday, including Alex Pereira vacating his light heavyweight title and Jiri Prochazka and Carlos Ulberg fighting for the vacant belt at UFC 327 in Miami! DC breaks down Pereira's likely move to heavyweight, and why he believes Jon Jones or Ciryl Gane HAVE TO BE the two potential opponents. Plus, Cormier breaks down Prochazka vs. Ulberg's MASSIVE FIGHT ahead. DC wants to know, who do YOU THINK Alex is going to fight next if he goes up to heavyweight: Gane or Jones? Let us know! articles.title: “You Make Money Quickly In MMA!💰” DJ And Volk On Making Money From MMA
How Do You Stop Chimaev? | Chael Fan Q&A

From contract exclusivity to negotiation myths to why adding more divisions won’t fix anything, this one covers it all. articles.title: If Pereira Moves Up… Vacate the Belt
articles.description: Alex Pereira appears headed to the White House card — and likely to heavyweight. But if he moves up, should he vacate? Chael breaks down the ripple effects of champions floating between divisions, Chimaev calling for big fights without defending, and why contenders like Oezdemir need to start making noise. Belt chaos is fun for fans — but dangerous for the locker room. articles.title: Islam should not face Michael Morales next...
articles.description: Kamaru Usman wanted Islam Makhachev — and the UFC told him they’re going in a different direction. If Usman can’t get the fight now, after dominating Joaquin Buckley, when will he ever get it? Chael breaks down the welterweight logjam, the Ian Garry and Michael Morales factor, and why this might be Usman’s last real window at a legacy fight. Time Stamps 0:00 – I Want Usman vs Islam 0:50 – Why This Fight Matters 1:40 – The Rankings Problem 2:15 – Ian Garry vs Morales 3:00 – The Rumor at MSG 3:40 – Rakhmonov’s Impact 4:10 – What Do You Do With Usman? 4:45 – Now or NeverBad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Bisping Got This One Wrong
articles.description: Michael Bisping suggested the winner of Max Holloway vs Charles Oliveira for the BMF belt could leapfrog the division and get a title shot. Chael disagrees. The BMF doesn’t need to be a golden ticket — it’s already meaningful. Instead of using it to skip contenders, why not build real lineage around it and stop pushing Arman Tsarukyan to the side? The lightweight division doesn’t need shortcuts — it needs clarity.
